title: verbascum phlomoides var. nemorosum
intro: Verbascum phlomoides var. nemorosum, also known as the woodland mullein, is a perennial plant belonging to the figwort family (Scrophulariaceae). This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Verbascum phlomoides var. nemorosum include its ability to soothe respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water or a solvent like ethanol.
